observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
The Inventory Committee to prepare a Property Inventory Plan (PIP) which contains the timing and scope of the PPE count, and conduct the actual count after the PIP is approved by the Municipal Mayor;
The Municipal Mayor to approve the PIP which serves as his concurrence to the Inventory Committee’s plan to conduct the PPE count;
After the PPE count, the Inventory Committee Chairman and its Member as well as the Municipal Mayor to sign the RPCPPE; and
The MAcco, the MT and the Inventory Committee to immediately reconcile their records and effect the necessary adjustments on the affected accounts in compliance with pertinent provisions of the NGAS for LGUs.
We recommended the following:
The MAcco and General Service Officer, reconcile the land account per books and per records and trace the existing CTs with the recorded ones for accurate valuation of land account. After which, the MAcco record the existing certificates of titles not recorded in the books pursuant to IPSAS; and
The Management with respect to unregistered parcels of land, prioritize the registration and transfer of the title of land in the name of LGU Bambang to have conclusive evidence of ownership and to fully protect the interest of the Municipality in adherence to Section 39(2) of PD No. 1445 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20 1992.
We recommended the MAcco to reclassify the errors to their appropriate PPE accounts as guided by the following:

We recommended that Management fully implement the projects that have been debited to the CIP accounts for a relatively long period of time for them to be reclassified to their appropriate accounts and subjected to depreciation to properly effect the Buildings’ and Infrastructure Assets’ wear and tear to attain a fairly valued FSs pursuant to Paragraph 14 of IPSAS 17 and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the MAcco install procedures to effectively monitor and promptly report projects undertaken and include in the Status of Implementation Report all projects which are yet to be implemented including those pertaining to the prior years.

We recommended the MAcco to reclassify the portion of the long-term loan, due for settlement within twelve months from the reporting date, as current liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended the MAcco to reconcile the noted difference of ₱1,059,867.30 existing on the two receivable accounts and furnish results of reconciliation or adjusting entries made to the Office of the Auditor.
We recommended the MAcco to recognize interest expenses and applicable charges imposed on its loans upon incurrence and ensure strict compliance with the accrual basis of accounting.

We recommended the MAcco to properly classify the accounts specified above which are aging to more than one year under the non-current asset and non-current liability portion of the Statement of Financial Position to present reliable data in the agency’s FS.

We recommended the MAcco to resolve the above-mentioned reconciling items and identify the cause of discrepancies in the Schedules to come up with a more accurate and reliable information.
Likewise, we recommended that Management identify the cause(s) of the deficiency and coordinate immediately with the GSIS to reconcile and update agency/employee records.
Further, if once reconciled and resulted to unremitted premiums, we recommended the MT to observe the prescribed due dates for the remittances of withheld taxes, premium contributions and loans in compliance to the aforementioned BIR, Pag-IBIG, PhilHealth and GSIS laws, rules and regulations in order to avoid incurrence of possible penalties.
Moreover, we recommended Management to require the MAcco and the MT to immediately submit the lacking DVs and its supporting documents, otherwise the transactions covered by the unsubmitted documents be suspended/disallowed in audit.

We recommended Management to submit a report on the extraordinary suggestion, invention, superior accomplishment and other personal effects of each individual who received the PRAISE Incentive Bonus including the computation of the savings generated or realized therefrom in which the Municipality has been benefited.

We recommended that Management cause the immediate refund of the medical allowance paid to its municipal officials, regular and casual employees and JOs in violation of R.A. No. 6758 and submit proof of refund thereof to the Audit Team for validation. Likewise, we recommended that Management only grant allowances and benefits authorized under the Act, subject to funds availability. Further, such other additional compensation not otherwise specified in R.A. No. 6758 may be granted as may be determined by the DBM.

We recommended Management to submit justification for why the amount of ₱2,272,351.07 incurred in the conduct of “Benchmarking or Enhancement Training Cum Team-Building” in Vitalis Villas, Ilocos Sur should not be disallowed in audit. We likewise recommended that Management strictly observe prudence in the management of its funds.

We recommended that Management provide its written justification on the legality and reasonableness of the expenditures. In addition, we recommended that Management stop granting financial assistance intended for private purposes and failure to do so shall result of these expenditures to audit disallowances.

We recommended that Management furnish to the Office of the Auditor its legal basis which authorizes it to disburse the limited funds of the government for SEI to its JOs, and to submit justification why it should not be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to immediately reclassify the completed infrastructure projects into the appropriate PPE accounts consistent with Paragraph 14 of IPSAS 17 and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015. Moreover, we recommended that Management, through the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant, to henceforth install procedures to effectively monitor and promptly report projects undertaken by administration.

We recommended that Management require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of the Property, Plant and Equipment. Moreover, we recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately reconcile their records and effect the necessary adjustments on the affected accounts in compliance with pertinent provisions of the NGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and update the PPELCs and PCs in accordance with pertinent provisions of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Agriculturist to:
submit the inventory of breeding stocks and other biological assets, and provide information on the prevailing market price for each item as of reporting date which shall be reflected in the financial statements; and
conduct inspection to locate and know the present condition and stewardship of these animals and furnish the Audit Team of the report thereon.

We recommended that Management require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of the Inventories and henceforth submit the required RPCI to the Audit Team within the prescribed deadline in strict adherence with Section 124, Chapter 7, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and update the SLCs and SCs in accordance with pertinent provisions of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to submit the report on the distribution of the relief packs duly signed by the recipients indicating acceptance/acknowledgement thereof to the Municipal Accountant and other documents pertinent thereto as basis in effecting adjustment/s to come up with a more reliable Inventories balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:
1. strictly comply with the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Cir. No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances;
2. require/demand the immediate settlement of all long overdue cash advances by issuing demand letter and if necessary withhold the salaries of the concerned officials and employees until the settlement thereof; and
3. reclassify the accounts with negative balances caused by excessive liquidations into Due to Officers and Employees.

We recommended that Management through the Municipal Accountant initiate the filing of request for the write off of dormant receivable accounts by securing the necessary documents as per Section 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016. Also, we recommended Management to issue demand letters for the enforcement of collections to former officials and employees who have known residences, otherwise secure a certification of no residence in the barangay of last known address which could be one of the bases to write-off the accounts. Lastly, we recommended Management to exert diligent and wise efforts to verify and substantiate the other receivable accounts with no supporting documents in order to support its validity.

We recommended that Management through the Municipal Accountant transfer and record the unspent balance of current year’s QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of P2,130,792.97 to the Trust Fund to reflect a more accurate financial position and performance of the Municipality and henceforth avoid that the unspent current year balance be recognized only on the next accounting year. In addition, we recommended that the unexpended balance of DRRM Fund for CY 2015 of P270,561.70, which had been available for five years in the Trust Fund, be reverted back to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

The MDDRMO has submitted the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund to the Audit Team.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
We will evaluate the submitted report and continuously monitor the full implementation of our audit recommendation as part of our constitutional mandate and as a vibrant and enabling partner in the achievement of the LGU’s objectives and goals.

We recommended that Management immediately remit the unexpended balance of PDAF aggregating to P677,186.30 to the Bureau of Treasury in compliance with the pronouncements of the Supreme Court in GR Nos. 208566, 208493 and 209251 and in view of Section 29(3), Article VI of the 1987 Philippine Constitution.

We recommended that the unexpended balances of fund transfers from Other LGUs and NGAs aggregating to P1,435,526.41 be reverted back to the source agencies so that it could be used for other beneficial projects pursuant to COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

We recommended that:
1. the Municipal Engineer prepare the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (GPPAs) and those that are to be implemented during the year which shall be signed by the Head of Agency and to be submitted to the office of the auditor;
2. the Municipal Mayor henceforth inform in writing the COA Audit Team Leader within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted; and
3. the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the required monthly Monitoring Report of GPPA following the format in Annex L of this Report.

We recommended that:
1. the Municipal Mayor terminate the designation order as D.Os of the aforementioned accountable officers who are already performing collections functions and instead designate new Disbursing Officers upon recommendation by the Municipal Treasurer;
2. the Accountable Officers to fully liquidate their outstanding cash advances in compliance with existing COA rules and regulations on grant of cash advances; and
3.  the Implementing Offices to henceforth consider public bidding in their respective Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) as the mode of procurement for meals and snacks to avoid granting of relatively large amount of cash advances.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere with Section 54.6(f) of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (RIRR) of RA 9184 and ensure that the LGU only transact with technically, legally and financially capable suppliers.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the lacking documentary requirements and henceforth ensure that all supporting documents identified by laws, rules, and regulations for each transaction are complete before certifying completeness of supporting documents in the disbursement vouchers.

We recommended that management strictly comply with the provisions of BIR Revenue Memorandum Order No. 23-2014 on the withholding of taxes to help the government in generating revenues to meet its appropriation requirement and to avoid penalties and legal action against the erring withholding agents from the BIR.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer cause the immediate marking of all motor vehicles with the word “For Official Use Only” (3 inches) with the name of the LGU Bambang and monitor the utilization of the motor vehicles as required under the Manual on the Audit of Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles.

We recommended for the immediate restitution of the said shortage and/or the submission of necessary documents and written explanation why such shortage occurred. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure collections are remitted intact daily and do not assign municipal collector with unremitted collection and monitor that all collectors with the collection responsibilities unless all previous collections are fully remitted.  Lastly, we recommended that the Liquidating Officer strictly monitor the remittance of collection and make the necessary demand to ensure remittance of all collections. Be it noted that failure of the accountable officer to remit such shortage shall constitute as basis for the auditor to refer the matter to the office of the Ombudsman for proper filing of appropriate charges for the acts committed and misappropriating the government funds.

observations.recommendation:
We  recommended  that  Management  undertake  a  complete
periodic physical  inventory  of  municipal  properties  on
scheduled date as mandated by law and immediately reconcile
the balances  of  any  affected  accounts  to  permit  fair
presentation of PPE in the financial statements.

Require/demand the immediate settlement of all long overdue
cash advances and if necessary withhold the salaries of the
concerned officials and employees until the settlement
thereof.

Comply strictly with the provisions of Section 89 of PD No.
1445 and COA Cir. No. 97 – 002 dated February 10,  1997  in
the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We  recommended  that  management   implement   effective
internal control over the billing and collection  processes
of the Bambang  Water  System  to  ensure  that  receivable
accounts are properly stated and the right revenue  due  to
the  Municipality  will  be  collected  and  utilized   for
government projects and  eventually  increase  the  earning
potential of the Municipality and avoid  incurring  further
losses.

(a)  Intensify  its  efforts  by  instituting   appropriate
measures to  ensure  collection  of  the  long  outstanding
receivables.

(b) Exert diligent efforts to verify and  substantiate  the
other receivable accounts with no supporting  documents  in
order to support its validity.

We recommended  that  management  maintain  complete  subsidiary
ledgers and prepare the respective ﬁnancial statements  of  each
special account in the General  Fund  to  permit  the  efficient
assessment of the proﬁtability and viability of operating  these
economic enterprises.

We  recommended  that  management  refrain  from  charging
expenditures against the 20% DF  which  are  not  allowable
under the above-mentioned guidelines  and  always  bear  in
mind  that  development  projects  partake  the  nature  of
investment or capital expenditures.

We recommended that development projects to be funded  out
of the 20% DF should be ultimately identified and specified
prior to the enactment of its  corresponding  appropriation
ordinance. Likewise, the equivalent  program  of  works  of
these projects should already have been prepared for proper
planning and costing  so  that  during  the  ensuing  year,
management will just have  to  implement  the  projects  as
planned. Also, fast-track the implementation of  continuing
development  projects  by  reconsidering  their   mode   of
procurement as indicated in the Annual Procurement Plan  or
through re-programming them to other  development  projects
that are feasible to be implemented.

We recommended that management prepare and submit the Report  on
Sources  and  Utilization  of   LDRRMF   as   required   by
regulations  to  ensure  proper  monitoring,  control   and
documentation of disaster funds.

We recommended that Management require the  suppliers  and
contractors to post warranty  security  to  cover  possible
expenses  from  manufacturing   and   structural   defects.
Management should also refrain from accepting  Surety  Bond
as a substitute for retention money if the project does not
incur delay and is satisfactorily undertaken.

We  recommended  that  Management  attach  complete
documentary requirements on their procurements and for  the
supplier to indicate dates therein.  Also,  we  recommended
that management ensure that the  contractor  or  supplier’s
receipt date on NTP be indicated for proper  monitoring  of
projects.

We recommended:

For the Accounting Office to strictly comply with the timeliness on the recording and submission of accounts as required under existing laws and regulations;

For the Concerned Departments to hasten/fast track the transmittal of all documents and reports to the Accounting Office within the most reasonable time to ensure that the latter could comply with regulation.

For Management to consider the possibility of augmenting the personnel of the Accounting from other departments, or allow overtime, especially during the preparation of reports, provided that there are available funds to support the endeavor.

observations.recommendation:
1.6   We  recommended  that  Management  undertake  a  complete
                 periodic physical  inventory  of  municipal  properties  on
                 scheduled date as mandated by law and immediately reconcile
                 the balances  of  any  affected  accounts  to  permit  fair
                 presentation of PPE in the financial statements.

2.7   We recommended that management:

                 Require/demand the immediate settlement of all long overdue
                 cash advances and if necessary withhold the salaries of the
                 concerned officials  and  employees  until  the  settlement
                 thereof.

                 Comply strictly with the provisions of Section 89 of PD No.
                 1445 and COA Cir. No. 97 – 002 dated February 10,  1997  in
                 the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

3.5   We recommended that management implement effective control
                 over the billing and  collection  process  of  the  Bambang
                 Water System such that reliable reports would be  forwarded
                 to the accounting department which would serve as  a  basis
                 to recognize the  receivable  in  the  books  of  accounts.
                 Lastly, we recommend  the  Municipality  to  intensify  the
                 collection of all outstanding receivables from customers.

4.6   We recommended that the Accounting Office record water  expenses
                 as incurred and management implement effective control over
                 collection process to ensure full collection from customers
                 to cover the cost of water distribution  and  employ  early
                 payment  and  settlement  of  accounts   with   the   water
                 distributor. Lastly, we recommend the bookkeeper to  adjust
                 the books  with  the  following  compound  entry  for  fair
                 presentation in the financial statements:


                 Water Expenses               P2,110,972.20
                 Prior Years’ Adjustments             8,352,546.60
                       Accounts Payable                  P10,463,518.80

5.8  We recommended that Management:

                 (a)  Intensify  its  efforts  by  instituting   appropriate
                 measures to  ensure  collection  of  the  long  outstanding
                 receivables.


                 (b) Exert diligent efforts to verify and  substantiate  the
                 other receivable accounts with no supporting  documents  in
                 order to support its validity.

6.5    We  recommended  that  management   implement   effective
                 internal control over the billing and collection  processes
                 of the Bambang  Water  System  to  ensure  that  receivable
                 accounts are properly stated and the right revenue  due  to
                 the  Municipality  will  be  collected  and  utilized   for
                 government projects.

7.4   We  recommended  that  management  refrain  from  charging
                 expenditures against the 20% DF  which  are  not  allowable
                 under the above-mentioned guidelines  and  always  bear  in
                 mind  that  development  projects  partake  the  nature  of
                 investment or capital expenditures.

8.7   We recommended that management fast track the construction
                 of sanitary landfill by employing additional equipment  and
                 manpower and strengthen its campaign of “reuse, reduce, and
                 recycle” or segregation at source .

9.5   We recommended that management prepare and submit the Report  on
                 Sources  and  Utilization  of   LDRRMF   as   required   by
                 regulations  to  ensure  proper  monitoring,  control   and
                 documentation of disaster funds.

15.4  We recommended for the immediate restitution of  the  said
                 shortage and/or the submission of  necessary  documents  or
                 written explanation why such shortage occurred.

16.4  We recommended that the  Collectors  and  the  Liquidating
                 Officer account/report/turnover the outstanding Accountable
                 Forms and deposit immediately the collections thereof.

17.4  We recommended that the Accountable Officer reconcile  his
                 cashbook with the Accounting ledgers  at  least  quarterly.
                 Discrepancies identified should be adjusted accordingly  to
                 come up with updated,  accurate  and  reliable  balance  of
                 accountabilities.

18.4  We recommended that the  concerned  official  prepare  the
                 Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms  with  Money
                 Value to  properly  establish  the  accountability  of  the
                 collectors and avoid misappropriation of collections.

19.3  We recommended that  Disbursing  Officer  update  her
                 fidelity  bond  to  cover  up  possible  risk  of  loss  of
                 government money.

20.3   We  recommended  that  the  Accountable  Officer  provide
                 reconciling items for petty cash fund to come up  with  the
                 same balance with the accounting records.

1.5   We recommended that Management  undertake  a  complete  periodic
           physical inventory of municipal properties on scheduled date  as
           mandated by law and immediately reconcile the  balances  of  any
           affected accounts to permit fair  presentation  of  PPE  in  the
           financial statements.

2.8   We recommended that management:

           1. Require/demand the immediate settlement of all  long  overdue
             cash advances and if necessary withhold the  salaries  of  the
             concerned  officials  and  employees  until   the   settlement
             thereof.



           2. Comply strictly with the provisions of Section 89 of  PD  No.
             1445 and COA Cir. No. 97 – 002 dated February 10, 1997 in  the
             granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

3.4    We  recommended  that  management  instruct  all  concerned  to
           conduct  physical  inventory  of   the   breeding   stocks   and
           immediately reconcile the  accounting  records  and  accordingly
           effect adjustments in the books, if any.

4.9  We recommended that Management:

           1. intensify its efforts by instituting appropriate measures  to
           ensure collection of the long outstanding receivables.


           2. exert diligent efforts to verify and substantiate  the  other
           receivable accounts amounting to P252,418.41 in order to support
           its validity.

5.6   We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer forward  a  copy  of
           list of the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible
           for the year based on the latter’s  Real  Property  Tax  Account
           Registers to the Municipal Accountant as a basis  in  setting-up
           deferred  Special  Education  Tax  and   Special   Education   Tax
           Receivable. Further, we recommended adjusting both said accounts
           for proper financial reporting.

6.6   We recommended the MCPC to hold meetings to formulate  projects,
           programs and activities concerning the welfare of  the  children
           especially the children at risk and children  in  conflict  with
           the law and ultimately to come up with Annual Work and Financial
           Plan (AWFP).


      6.7   Further, we recommended that  the  Management  adhere  with  the
           allocation of prescribed rate of 1% of IRA for the  programs  of
           the MCPC, see to it that the amount allocated are disbursed  for
           strengthening and implementation of the  programs  of  MCPC  and
           programs for the children in conflict  with  the  law  are  also
           prioritized.

7.5    We  recommended  that  the  development  programs/projects  and
           activities incorporated in the Annual Investment Plan  (AIP)  be
           aligned with the provisions under Section 3  of  DILG-DBM  Joint
           Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22,  2017.  Hence,
           the above-mentioned projects totaling P2,500,000.00 be realigned
           to development projects which are qualified under  the  20%  DF.
           Likewise, we recommended that management refrain  from  charging
           expenditures against the 20% DF which are  not  allowable  under
           the above-mentioned guidelines  and  that  development  projects
           partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.

8.6     We recommended that the barangay  development  projects  to  be
           funded by the MDAF be specifically identified  in  the  approved
           budget in order to forestall improper utilization of the 20% DF.
           This will ensure that development funds are utilized to  support
           the Philippine Development Plan and Public Investment Program.

9.6   We recommended that the LDDRMIP be prepared in  accordance  with
           the format prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002. Likewise,
           we recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and certify
           correct a monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF
           and for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer
           (LDRRMO) to submit said report on or before the 15th  day  after
           the end of each month through the LDRRMC and  Local  Development
           Council (LDC) to the COA Auditor of the LGU.

10.6  We recommended that the concerned officials and LSB  members  of
           the municipality prepare and approve  the  SEF  Budget  for  the
           ensuing year within the timeline as set forth in Section 5.5  of
           Deped-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 so  that  the  fund
           would be utilized as planned or as needed.

11.6  We recommended that management evaluate the  components  of  the
           fund balance maintained under its SEF account to  determine  the
           actual unexpended balance thereof and ensure  its  inclusion  in
           the SEF budget in the ensuing year.

12.5  We recommended that management prepare an APP which includes all
           procurements of the municipality. The mode of  procurement  must
           also be indicated in the APP for each transaction.  Revision  in
           the  procurement  such  as  the  mode  of  procurement  must  be
           reflected in the APP and a BAC  Resolution  must  be  issued  to
           effect such change.

13.9  We recommended that management (1) continuously comply with  its
           commitment as  regards  the  recommendations  of  the  concerned
           agency; and (2) fast track implementation of the development and
           construction of the  SLF  considering  that  the  loan  for  the
           purpose was already granted;  (3)  suspend  the  utilization  of
           temporary open pit as disposal area and  instead  segregate  the
           waste and temporarily store in sacks to avoid  contamination  of
           land; and (4) strictly implement “no segregation, no collection”
           policy or segregation at source.

           from the PHilHealth in accordance with the  PhilHealth  Circular
           No. 20, s. 2012 and see to it that the medicines and drugs to be
           acquired  is  in  harmony  with  the  Philippine  National  Drug
           Formulary (PNDF). The MHO should also make an  estimate  on  the
           quantity and kind of medicines to be bought which  should  cater
           to the needs of their patients.

           medicines and drugs and the P164,594.00 overdraft be replenished
            from succeeding proceeds from the PhilHealth  coming  from  the
           40% allocation for reagents, medical supplies and equipment.

           1.   stop  the  granting  of  cash  advances  to  Officials  and
           employees of the LGU with unliquidated cash advance.

              advances.


           3.    be cognizant with the existing regulations on the granting
              of cash advances and to be strict in  the  implementation  of

           days from date of their receipt of the Annual Audit  Report  for
           CY 2017.

observations.recommendation:
1.5   We recommended that Management  undertake  a  complete  periodic
           physical inventory of municipal properties on scheduled date  as
           mandated by law and immediately reconcile the  balances  of  any
           affected accounts to permit fair  presentation  of  PPE  in  the
           financial statements.

2.8   We recommended that management:

           1. require/demand the immediate settlement of all  long  overdue
             cash advances and if necessary withhold the  salaries  of  the
             concerned  officials  and  employees  until   the   settlement
             thereof.


           2. comply strictly with the provisions of Section 89 of  PD  No.
             1445 and COA Cir. No. 97 – 002 dated February 10, 1997 in  the
             granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

3.5 We recommended that Management:

           1.   stop  the  granting  of  cash  advances  to  Officials  and
              employees of the LGU with unliquidated cash advance.


           2.    cause the immediate settlement of these long overdue  cash
              advances.


           3.    be cognizant with the existing regulations on the granting
              of cash advances and to be strict in  the  implementation  of
              the same.

4.4    We  recommended  that  management  instruct  all  concerned  to
           conduct  physical  inventory  of   the   breeding   stocks   and
           immediately reconcile the  accounting  records  and  accordingly
           effect adjustments in the books, if any.

8.9         We recommended that Management:

           1. intensify its efforts by instituting appropriate measures  to
             ensure collection of the long outstanding receivables.


           2. exert diligent efforts to verify and substantiate  the  other
             receivable accounts  amounting  to  P250,418.41  in  order  to
             support its validity.

9.4   We   recommended   that   management   fast   tract   the
           implementation of all on – going and not yet started  programmed
           and funded projects under the 20% Development Fund  to  optimize
           utilization thereof and timely brings the  desired  benefits  to
           the intended beneficiaries.

10.5  We  recommended  that  programmed  PPAs  under  the   20%
           Development Fund be utilized for capital/investment  expenditure
           pursuant to the provisions of the DILG and DBM Joint  Memorandum
           Circular No. 2011 – 1.

11.4   We  recommended  management  to  continuously  ensure   proper
           utilization of the Special Education Fund  conformably  with  RA
           No. 5447, Section 272 of  LGC  and  DECS,  DBM  and  DILG  Joint
           Circular Nos. 1 s. 1998 and 01-A s. 2000.

12.5  We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer forward  a  copy  of
           certified list of the name of taxpayers and the amount  due  and
           collectible for the year based on the latter’s Real Property Tax
           Account Registers to the Municipal  Accountant  as  a  basis  in
           setting-up deferred Special Education Tax and Special  Education
           Tax Receivable.

13.5  We recommended that Management effect the adjustment to  reflect
           the accurate balances of the accounts pertaining to the  Special
           Education Fund and to account properly in the future collections
           of additional 1% RPT  in  accordance  with  accepted  accounting
           principles.

14.7  We recommended that management adhere with COA Circular No. 2012-
           02 on the  timely  submission  of  the  Report  on  Sources  and
           Utilization of DRRMF.

15.4   We  recommended  that  management  ensure  that  all   budgeted
           projects,  programs  and  activities  are  optimally  and  fully
           implemented.

16.5  We recommended that management (1) continuously comply with  its
           commitment as  regards  the  recommendations  of  the  concerned
           agency; and (2) fast tract compliance of  the  requirements  for
           the immediate implementation of the development and construction
           of the SLF considering that the loan for the purpose was already
           granted.

17.5  We recommended that management stop the payment of terminal  pay
           to personnel who are not yet separated from service.

18.5  We recommended that Management collect the agency fee  due  from
           non-members of CNA who were paid with CNA benefits and remit the
           same to the negotiating agent of the LGU.

19.3  We recommended that management strictly observe the provision of
           Section 361 par. (e) and (g) of GAAM Volume I on the  submission
           of mandatory reports, relative to the use of government vehicles
           and henceforth.

20.4  We recommended  that  management  maintain  complete  subsidiary
           ledgers and prepare the respective ﬁnancial statements  of  each
           special account in the General  Fund  to  permit  the  efficient
           assessment of the proﬁtability and viability of operating  these
           economic enterprises.

21.5  We recommended that management immediately  prepare  and  submit
           its Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI).

22.5   We  recommended  that  the  concerned  municipal  employee  be
           cognizant of the  deadlines  relative  to  the  registration  of
           municipal vehicles in order to avoid unnecessary surcharges  and
           other forms of penalties.

23.5  We recommended that management revert  the  unutilized  PDAF  to
           the account of the National Treasury Office.

24.5  We recommended:


          1.  for  the  Accounting  Office  to  strictly  comply  with  the
             timeliness on the recording  and  submission  of  accounts  as
             required under existing laws and regulations;


          2. for the Treasury Office to hasten/fast tract  the  transmittal
             of all documents and reports to the Accounting  Office  within
             the most reasonable time  to  ensure  that  the  latter  could
             comply with regulation.


          3. for Management to consider the possibility of  augmenting  the
             personnel of the Accounting and Treasury  Offices  from  other
             departments, especially during the preparation of  reports  or
             allow overtime, provided that there  are  available  funds  to
             support the endeavor.

25.3  We recommended that management submit to the auditor within  the
           prescribed period all perfected contracts, purchase  orders  and
           the like including copies of documents in support thereof.

26.1  We commend the Accounting Office for timely  submission  of  the
           LGU’s  Financial  Statements  and  we  further   recommend   the
           continuous observance in the succeeding reporting periods.
